36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.

42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54

68

80	   - Read the whole file.

82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.

79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. 81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.

91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. 376	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
377	sheafification localizer
378	
379	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
380	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
381	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
382	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
383	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
384	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
385	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
386	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
387	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
388	structure. -/

390	section FlatWhisker
391	
392	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
393	
394	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
395	
396	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
397	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
398	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
399	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul

400	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
401	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
402	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
403	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
404	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
405	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
406	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
407	
408	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
409	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
410	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
411	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
412	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
413	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
414	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
415	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
416	
417	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
418	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
419	half. -/
420	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
421	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
422	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
423	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
424	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
425	  constructor
426	  intro U s
427	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
428	  | zero =>
429	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
430	      intro V i _
431	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
432	  | tmul a b =>
433	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
434	      intro V i hi
435	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
436	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
437	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
438	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
439	      congr 1
440	  | add s t hs ht =>
441	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
442	      intro V i hi
443	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
444	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
445	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
446	
447	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
448	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
449	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
450	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
451	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
452	injectivity half. -/
453	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
454	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
455	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
456	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
457	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
458	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
459	  constructor
460	  intro X ξ η h
461	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
462	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
463	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
464	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
465	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
466	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
467	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
468	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
469	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
470	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
471	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
472	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
473	  have hex : Function.Exact
474	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
475	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
476	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
477	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
478	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
479	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
480	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
481	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
482	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
483	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
484	    intro ζ
485	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
486	    | zero =>
487	        rw [map_zero]
488	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
489	    | tmul a kk =>
490	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
491	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
492	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
493	          rw [map_zero]
494	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
495	          exact kk.2
496	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
497	        intro V f hf
498	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
499	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
500	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
501	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
502	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
503	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
504	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
505	        rw [map_add]
506	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
507	        intro V f hf
508	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
509	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
510	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
511	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
512	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
513	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
514	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
515	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
516	  intro V f hf
517	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
518	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
519	  exact hf

522	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
523	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
524	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
525	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again

530	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
531	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
532	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
533	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
534	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
535	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
536	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
537	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,

540	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
541	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
542	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
543	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
544	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
545	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
546	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
547	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
548	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
549	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
550	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
551	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
552	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
553	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
554	  have hconj : g ▷ F
555	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
556	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,

558	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
559	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
560	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
561	  exact hwl

564	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
565	
566	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE

570	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
571	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves

574	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
575	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*

577	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points

661	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
662	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
663	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
664	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)

666	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
667	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
668	  have hconj : g ▷ F
669	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
670	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
671	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
672	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
673	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
674	  exact hwl

679	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
680	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
681	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
682	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
683	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
684	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)

687	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
688	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
689	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
690	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
691	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
692	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
693	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
694	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
695	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf

700	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
701	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
702	
703	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
704	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
705	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
706	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
707	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
708	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
709	
710	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
711	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
712	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
713	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
714	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
715	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
716	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
717	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
718	
719	section StalkLinearMap
720	
721	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
722	
723	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
724	
725	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
726	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
727	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
728	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
729	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
730	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
731	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
732	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
733	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
734	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
735	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
736	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
737	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
738	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
739	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
740	  map_smul' r s := by
741	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
742	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
743	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
744	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
745	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
746	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
747	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
748	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
749	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
750	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
751	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
752	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
753	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
754	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
755	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
756	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
757	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
758	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
759	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
760	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
761	      intro z
762	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
763	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
764	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
765	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
766	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
767	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
768	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
769	
770	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
771	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
772	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
773	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
774	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
775	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
776	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
777	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
778	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
779	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
780	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
781	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
782	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
783	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
784	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
785	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
786	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
787	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
788	
789	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
790	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
791	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
792	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
793	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
794	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
795	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
796	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
797	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
798	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
799	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
800	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
801	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
802	
803	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
804	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
805	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
806	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
807	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
808	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
809	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
810	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
811	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
812	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
813	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
814	
815	end StalkLinearMap
816	
817	end PresheafOfModules

980	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
981	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
982	
983	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
984	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
985	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
986	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
987	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
988	
989	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
990	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
991	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
992	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
993	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
994	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
995	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
996	
997	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
998	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
999	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
1000	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
1001	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
1002	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
1003	project-local supplement. -/
1004	
1005	namespace InternalHom

1007	open CategoryTheory Limits
1008	
1009	universe vC uC
1010	
1011	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1012	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

1014	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1015	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1016	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1017	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1018	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1019	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1020	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1021	
1022	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1023	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1024	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1025	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1026	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1027	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1028	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1029	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1030	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1031	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1032	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1033	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1034	
1035	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1036	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1037	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1038	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1039	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1040	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1041	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1042	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1043	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1044	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1045	    (by
1046	      intro X Y g; ext m
1047	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1048	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1049	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)

1050	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1051	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1052	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)

1054	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}

1056	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1057	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1058	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1059	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1060	
1061	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1062	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1063	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1064	
1065	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1066	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1067	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1068	
1069	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1070	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1071	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1072	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1073

1075	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1076	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1077	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1078	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1079	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1080	
1081	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1082	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1083	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1084	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1085	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1086	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1087	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1088	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1089	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1090	@[implicit_reducible]
1091	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1092	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1093	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1094	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1095	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1096	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1097	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1098	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1099	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1100	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1101	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1102	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1103	  add_smul f g φ := by
1104	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1105	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]

1107	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1108	
1109	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of

1111	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1112	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object

1114	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`

1117	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1118	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1119	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1120	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1121	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1122	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1123	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1124	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1125	
1126	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1127	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1128	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1129	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1130	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1131	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1132	@[implicit_reducible]
1133	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1134	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1135	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1136	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal

1139	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1140	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules

1142	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1143	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,

1149	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1150	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ

1157	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1158	  ext1 X; rfl

1199	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`

1202	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):

1204	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1205	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1206	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1207	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1208	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))

1210	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1211	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1212	  ext1 Y
1213	  ext m
1214	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1215	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1216	  congr 1
1217	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1218	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1219	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1220	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1221	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1222	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1223	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1224	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))

1226	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1227	    rw [e1, e2]
1228	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1229	  rw [hmor]
1230	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1231	  rfl

1237	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1238	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where

1240	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1241	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g

1246	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1247	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1248	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1249	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1250	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1251	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1252	  map_id X := by
1253	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1254	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1255	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1256	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1257	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1258	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1259	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ

1262	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1263	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1264	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module

1269	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))

1271	    restrictionMap f.unop
1272	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1273	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1274	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1275	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)

1277	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1278	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]

1282	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1283	
1284	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1285	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1286	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1287	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1288	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1289	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1290	
1291	namespace InternalHom
1292	
1293	section Assembly
1294	
1295	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1296	
1297	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1298	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1299	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1300	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1301	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1302	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1303	noncomputable def internalHom
1304	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1305	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1306	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1307	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1308	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1309	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1310	
1311	end Assembly
1312	
1313	end InternalHom

1321	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1322	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1323	
1324	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
1325	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
1326	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
1327	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/

1512	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1513	
1514	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1515	
1516	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1517	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1518	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1519	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1520	
1521	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1522	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1523	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1524	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1525	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1526	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1527	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1528	
1529	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1530	
1531	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1532	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1533	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1534	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1535	
1536	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1537	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1538	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1539	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1540	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1541	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1542	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1543	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1544	
1545	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1546	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1547	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1548	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1549	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1550	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1551	law is definitional. -/
1552	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1553	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

1555	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1556	
1557	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1558	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1559	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1560	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1561	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1562	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1563	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1564	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1565	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1566	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1567	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1568	
1569	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1570	
1571	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1572	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1573	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1574	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1575	
1576	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1577	
1578	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1579	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1580	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1581	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1582	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1583	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1584	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1585	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1586	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1587	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1588	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1589	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1590	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

1592	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1593	sheaf.**
1594	
1595	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1596	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1597	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly

1598	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1599	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1600	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1601	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1602	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1603	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1604	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1605	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app

1616	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫

1619	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1620	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)

1623	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :

1625	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1626	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1627	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M

1629	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
1630	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
1631	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
1632	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
